children.heading: Penalty amounts
children.label: 1 Penalty amounts
children.text: Any person that is determined, in accordance with paragraph (2), to have violated or shall be required by order to pay a civil penalty in an amount not to exceed $25,000 for each violation. For the purposes of this paragraph, each day during which a violation of continues shall constitute a separate violation of that section.section 8124 of this titlesection 8141 of this titlesection 8124 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before imposing a penalty against a person under paragraph (1), the head of an executive agency designated under shall provide the person with notice of the order. If, within 15 days after receiving the notice, the person requests a hearing, the head of the designated executive agency shall initiate a hearing on the violation.section 8111(a) of this title

children.heading: Conduct of hearing
children.label: B Conduct of hearing
children.text: Any hearing so requested shall be conducted before an administrative judge. The hearing shall be conducted in accordance with the requirements of . If no hearing is so requested, the order imposed by the head of the designated agency shall constitute a final agency action.section 554 of title 5

children.heading: Issuance of orders
children.label: C Issuance of orders
children.text: If the administrative judge determines, upon the preponderance of the evidence received, that a person named in the complaint has violated or , the administrative judge shall state the findings of fact and conclusions of law, and issue and serve on such person an order described in paragraph (1).section 8124 of this titlesection 8141 of this title

children.heading: Factors for determination of penalty amounts
children.label: D Factors for determination of penalty amounts
children.text: In determining the amount of any civil penalty, the administrative judge or the head of the designated agency shall take into account the nature, circumstances, extent, and gravity of the violation or violations and, with respect to the violator, the ability to pay, effect on ability to continue to do business, any history of such violations, the degree of culpability, the existence of an internal compliance program, and such other matters as justice may require.

children.label: i
children.text: set forth the time, date, and specific nature of the alleged violation or violations; and

children.label: ii
children.text: specify the administrative and judicial remedies available to the person or persons subject to the order, including the availability of a hearing and subsequent appeal.

children.heading: Administrative appellate review
children.label: 3 Administrative appellate review
children.text: The decision and order of an administrative judge shall be the recommended decision and order and shall be referred to the head of the designated executive agency for final decision and order. If, within 60 days, the head of the designated executive agency does not modify or vacate the decision and order, it shall become a final agency action under this subsection.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 4 Judicial review
children.text: A person adversely affected by a final order may, within 30 days after the date the final order is issued, file a petition in the Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit or in the Court of Appeals for the district in which the violation occurred.

children.label: i
children.text: the person has not filed a petition for judicial review of the order in accordance with paragraph (4), or

children.label: ii
children.text: a court in an action brought under paragraph (4) has entered a final judgment in favor of the designated executive agency,

children.heading: No review
children.label: B No review
children.text: In any such civil action, the validity and appropriateness of the final order shall not be subject to review.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: C Interest
children.text: Payment of penalties assessed in a final order under this section shall include interest at currently prevailing rates calculated from the date of expiration of the 60-day period referred to in paragraph (3) or the date of such final order, as the case may be.

children.heading: Criminal
children.label: b Criminal
children.text: Any person who violates or may, in addition to or in lieu of any civil penalty which may be imposed under subsection (a) for such violation, be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than five years, or both.section 8124 of this titlesection 8141 of this title
